nodes.title: Proof-neutral two-plane architecture
nodes.summary: A semantic plane defines exact ordered ZRM meaning; replaceable evidence backends authenticate it and cannot create commit authority.

nodes.title: Strongly associative accounting
nodes.summary: Monotone debit and credit limbs remove signed intermediate-overflow dependence on proof-tree shape while retaining cancelling coverage.

nodes.title: Context-indexed recursive paths
nodes.summary: Ordinary steps preserve validation context; rare governed bridge steps change policy, verifier, registry, cost, or proof-adapter context.

nodes.title: Deterministic serial refinement
nodes.summary: Parallel work remains nonsemantic and must refine authenticated preset-order execution through exact sealed point and range observations.

nodes.title: Privacy envelope with typed governance
nodes.summary: A candidate fixed outer proof can aim to hide inner logic identity while binding typed registry membership, nonrevocation, the exact statement, cost classes, and an explicit leakage budget.

nodes.title: Semantic/proof identity firewall
nodes.summary: Economic and replay identity follows the semantic segment; proof identity separately records backend, release, control root, children, and topology.

nodes.title: Proof-carrying research promotion
nodes.summary: Immutable challenge-bound evidence receipts feed a four-valued versioned policy decision with an orthogonal assurance vector.

nodes.title: Acyclic state and history roots
nodes.summary: Machine state and append-only history are sibling commitments advanced atomically and anchored independently against rollback and forks.

nodes.title: Typed registry forest
nodes.summary: Typed roots or type-indexed registry views for policies, proof images, adapters, cost models, and acceleration data help prevent cross-role substitution and make upgrade coupling explicit.

nodes.title: Partial or signed fold definedness
nodes.summary: A valid manifest can become valid or invalid according to parenthesization when the recursive carrier excludes identities or checks cancelling signed intermediates.

nodes.title: Nullifier-only parallel safety
nodes.summary: Distinct nullifiers and even disjoint writes do not prevent read-write skew, range phantoms, policy conflicts, or incompatible post-roots.

nodes.title: Receipt or accumulator authority escalation
nodes.summary: A valid conditional proof or running accumulator does not establish durable commit, finality, data availability, or accepted-journal authority.

nodes.title: Program-identity linkability
nodes.summary: Public uncommon logic identifiers, cost, size, timing, roots, and error classes can reveal supposedly private program choice.

nodes.title: Proof malleability and release drift
nodes.summary: Alternative proof encodings or flawed curve/linkage implementations can break replay accounting or soundness even when paper-level folding semantics look correct.

nodes.title: Research evidence laundering
nodes.summary: Mutable status, producer booleans, replay strings, signed provenance, or a successful Lean process can be mistaken for semantic proof.

nodes.title: Research Kernel nonreplay
nodes.summary: Incomplete events, DB/JSONL dual writes, nondeterminism, mutating reads, and incomplete exports make the current kernel an index rather than authoritative evidence.

nodes.title: Cyclic history commitment
nodes.summary: A journal committing a post-state that itself commits a history leaf derived from that journal forms a hash dependency cycle.

nodes.title: Keep exact mutation and refinement oracles
Defer selection of a folding backend
Defer recursive proofs over the evidence graph
Reject scheduler or proof receipts as commit authority
Reject one undifferentiated accumulator operation and mutable scalar status

edges.rationale: Accumulation separates running evidence from a final decision.
Guest verification creates assumptions that require resolution.
The proof-neutral semantic contract is the two-plane architecture.
Evidence type-state prevents conditional proof escalation.
Separate semantic algebras narrow each proof relation.
Monotone limbs eliminate cancelling signed intermediate overflow.
Lean proves the counterexample and replacement properties.
Accounting receives a strongly associative carrier.
Accounting is one typed effect algebra, not the journal algebra.
Non-uniform IVC motivates explicit typed routing and common state shape.
Authorization meaning depends on explicit state and time.
The bounded model accepts a typed bridge and rejects same-context-only composition.
Upgrades become explicit indexed arrows.
Private policy receipts need governed meaning across upgrades.
Preset-order refinement rejects write skew deterministically.
Sealed point and range observations expose hidden dependencies.
Serial refinement needs complete validation dependencies.
All explored schedules equal the sequential oracle.
All seeded point and range substitutions reject; complete decision roots also distinguish nullifier-guard and candidate-invariant dependencies omitted by program-only roots.
Write skew is a standard snapshot-isolation anomaly.
Authenticated range results need completeness material.
Scheduler work refines canonical semantics.
Exact reads make conflict validation meaningful.
I-confluence is profile-specific; canonical order remains default.
Only resolved and decided evidence crosses the fact boundary.
Proof and scheduler receipts cannot construct durable acceptance.
Accepted journals remain postcommit phase values.
The common envelope targets public program identity.
Hidden inner logic still needs typed governance and nonrevocation.
Privacy is stated only modulo an explicit leakage budget.
A common outer proof can hide inner predicate proof material.
The audit records public uncommon program-identity linkability.
Private envelopes need explicit governance and leakage contracts.
Role separation is enforced in authenticated state.
Acceleration data is one typed registry role.
Semantic replay identity is isolated from proof encoding and topology.
Paper and implementation security can diverge; malleability and cycle linkage require review.
Economic and audit identities become distinct.
Adaptive proof topology must remain outside semantic identity.
Promotion consumes typed receipts rather than producer assertions.
Assurance dimensions remain independent.
Eligible evidence receipts derive independent support and refutation bits.
Per-claim policy selects required assurance coordinates.
Deterministic reconstructive state provides a stable checkpoint for promotion.
Promotion becomes proof-carrying and policy-derived.
Contested and stale evidence remains explicit.
Transactional EventV2 and deterministic replay replace dual authority logs.
Three MCP runs exposed current event, graph, and promotion limits.
Deterministic receipt semantics must precede recursive proof compression.
Sibling roots remove the journal/post-state/history cycle.
State and history advance atomically but remain separate claims.
Append-only history requires externally observed consistency to detect rollback and forks.
HyperNova and ProtoStar routing models should share one ContextV1 corpus before selection.
Parallel PCD and multi-folding follow correct binary semantics.
Arity and CycleFold remain implementation choices.
The semantic relation remains independent of proof selection.
Each relation receives its own exact typed algebra.
Bounded mutation models found concrete false-safe schedules.
Serial-refinement and footprint mutants provide deterministic regressions.
ESSO produces replayable semantic counterexamples for seeded disasters.
One undifferentiated operation cannot be assumed to preserve every algebra's laws.
One scalar cannot preserve independent assurance dimensions.

non_claims: Graph edges express research support, dependency, test, or decision rationale; they are not formal logical entailment.
Source and experiment nodes resolve through the pinned registries and retain their recorded limitations.
The graph is a deterministic curated projection because the current Research Kernel export is not replay-complete.
